title: The Tiny Prince and the Jolly Teapot

pages.text: Once upon a time, in a bright, shiny castle, there lived a tiny prince named <name>. One sunny morning, he tiptoed into the royal kitchen and spotted a little teapot with sparkling eyes.
“Hello!” said the teapot, tipping a tiny bow. “I’m Tippy! But oh no, I keep spilling tea whenever someone is unkind.”
<name> giggled. He wanted to help. He watched as a grumpy cook said, “Move!” and—splash!—tea leapt from Tippy’s spout and danced all over the floor.
“Don’t worry, Tippy,” said <name>. “I’ll teach everyone to be kind!”
Soon, <name> held tea-time lessons. “Say ‘please’ and ‘thank you,’” he told the cooks, the maids, and even the royal guard. Each kind word made Tippy’s tea sparkle and shimmer.
Magic happened too—tea twirled like tiny dancers, cookies giggled when lifted from the tray, and little cups clapped for everyone who was polite.

Every corner of the castle filled with laughter. The teapot stopped spilling, and its smile grew wide and warm. <name> felt proud as the castle shimmered with kindness, and even the tiniest mouse waved a polite paw.
And from that day on, everyone remembered to be gentle and courteous, and Tippy the teapot always poured happy, twirling tea.
